steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.sanis: 'sanis' is an adjective meaning 'the healthy' (dative plural)

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere' — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.perfect: Perfect of 'esse' would be 'fuit' — 'est' is present

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are principal parts of 'edo' (to eat)
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are principal parts of 'sto' (to stand)
hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are principal parts of 'fero' (to carry)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'medicum' — 'Medicus' is the nominative subject
hints.vocative: Vocative would be 'medice' — 'Medicus' is nominative subject here

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'medici' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'medici' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'medici' ends in -i

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'sani' — 'sanis' is dative/ablative plural
hints.ablative: 'sanis' could be ablative plural, but here it is dative (necessary FOR the healthy)
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'sanorum' — 'sanis' is dative plural

translation: A physician is not necessary for the healthy
